folio.label: 122v
folio.l: And for they han I knowen it , so fern~
Therfore cessetú here iangelyngö , & here wonder
As sore wonder some , on cause of thonder
On ebbe and flode , on gossomä & on myst
And on alle thynge , til æ the cause is wist
Thus iangle they , & demen & deuyse
Til that the kyngö , gan fro the bord arise
¢ Phebus hatú lost , the angle mediornal
And ©et ascendyngö , was the bestã roial
The gentil leou~ , witú his aldiran
Whan that this tartre kyngö , Cambyuscan
Ros fro his bord , there as he sat ful heye
Bifore hi~ gotú , the loude mynstralcye
Til he come to his chambre , of parementz
There as there sounen , diuäse Instrumentz
That it is like an heuene , for to here
Now dauncen lusty venus , children dere
For in the fissú , here lady sat ful heye
And loketú on hem , witú a frendlicú eye
This noble kyngö , is set vp on his Trone
This straunge knygút , is to hi~ fet ful sone
¢ And on the daunce he gotú , witú Canacee
Here is the reuel , and the Iolitee
That is nat able , a dul man to deuyse
He must han knowen loue , and his seruyse
And ben a feestlicú man , as fressú as May
That shulde ©ow deuysen , swicú a ray
Who coude telle ©ow , the foårme of daunces
So vncoutú , and swicú fressú countenaunces
Swicú subtil lokyngö , and dissimulynges
For drede , of gelous me~nes aparceyuynges
Noman but launcelot , and he is ded
Therfore I passe , of al this lustied
I sey nomore , but in this Iolynesse
I lete hem , til men to the Soper dresse
The Styward , bit spices for to hye
And eke the wyn , and al this melodye
The vsshers , and the Squyery is gon
The spices and the wyn , is come anon
They ete & drynke , and whan this had an ende
Vn to the temple , as resou~ was , they wende
¢ The seruyse don they , & soupen al be day
What nedetú to ©ow , rehercen hereheereheere , here array
Eche man wot wel , that a kynges fest
Hatú plentee to the meest , and to the leest
